page: Amazon Web Services g7e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, driven by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor operating at 2.4 GHz. The CPU includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

For accelerated workloads, the g7e.2xlarge includes a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This hardware combination offers a high-density resource profile, balancing high-capacity GPU memory with fast local storage and high-speed networking.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-threaded tasks, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip compression reaching the top 10% tier. LLM inference benchmarks also demonstrate top-tier capabilities, processing prompts at 7273.65 tokens/second and generating text at 222.04 tokens/second on a medium 7B model. Multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for graphics-intensive applications, large language model inference, and workloads requiring substantial GPU memory alongside fast local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive, GPU-accelerated virtual server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory. The hardware configuration is highlighted by a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM, alongside 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance and exceptional LLM inference speeds, processing 7B model prompts at over 7,200 tokens per second. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. This resource profile is highly optimized for graphics-intensive applications, machine learning inference, and database workloads requiring high-capacity VRAM and fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an Intel Xeon CPU, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, a 96 GB NVIDIA Blackwell GPU, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for intensive graphics and LLM workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.2xlarge is a Nitro-based instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, a 96 GB NVIDIA Blackwell GPU, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring high-capacity VRAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and fast local NVMe storage for intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with AMX support
Single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM
64 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier single-core CPU and LLM inference benchmark performance
Optimized for graphics-intensive applications, LLM inference, and database workloads
